file_analyses.file_purpose: UI metadata file containing comprehensive element definitions for the HP Instant Ink enrollment page. Captures all interactive elements on the enrollment/review page including plan cards, shipping information, billing details, and action buttons with their locators, attributes, and properties. Used by the test automation framework to identify and interact with UI elements during enrollment flow testing.

page_info.title: HP Instant Ink – Ink Monthly Subscription | HP Official Site

interactive_elements.inferred_purpose: Container div displaying the selected Instant Ink plan details on the enrollment review page
Button to edit or modify the selected Instant Ink plan during enrollment review
Container div displaying shipping address information for Instant Ink cartridge delivery
Button to edit or modify the shipping address for Instant Ink cartridge delivery
Container div displaying billing and payment information for Instant Ink subscription
Button to edit or modify billing and payment information for Instant Ink subscription
Primary action button to proceed with enrollment after reviewing plan, shipping, and billing details. Disabled until all required information is complete.
Button to add billing and payment information when no billing details are present
Link to open a dialog or form for entering promotional codes or PIN codes to apply discounts to the Instant Ink subscription
Tooltip or information icon associated with the billing card providing additional help or context about billing
Link to claim a special offer or enrollment kit code during the Instant Ink enrollment process
Link to view detailed information about Instant Ink benefits and features

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This JSON file contains UI metadata for the HP Instant Ink enrollment review page. It captures 12 interactive elements with comprehensive locator information including data-testid attributes (preferred), CSS selectors, and XPath selectors. The elements represent the key components of the enrollment flow: plan selection card, shipping information card, billing information card, and action buttons for editing each section and continuing enrollment. The file includes page_info with URL, title, and timestamp. All elements have multiple selector strategies providing fallback options for test automation. The data-testid selectors are the most reliable and should be preferred. Elements are marked with form_priority and auth_priority flags to indicate their importance in different test scenarios. The Continue button is marked as disabled (is_enabled: false) indicating it requires all enrollment steps to be completed before activation.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Comprehensive UI metadata file for the HP Instant Ink home/landing page and account dashboard. Contains detailed element definitions for navigation, account management, subscription cancellation flow, plan selection, cookie consent, and footer links. Provides multiple locator strategies (data-testid, CSS, XPath, text-based) for each interactive element to support robust test automation across different scenarios and page states.

page_info.title: HP Instant Ink – Ink Monthly Subscription | HP Official Site

Header navigation link to Frequently Asked Questions page about Instant Ink service
Button to access account management features, likely triggers sign-in if not authenticated

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This comprehensive JSON metadata file contains 30 interactive elements from the HP Instant Ink home page and account dashboard. It includes critical elements for test automation including subscription cancellation flow (Cancel Instant Ink, Confirm Cancellation, Keep Instant Ink buttons), plan selection (Select Monthly button), cookie consent management (I ACCEPT, MORE OPTIONS buttons), and extensive footer navigation links. The file provides multiple locator strategies for each element with data-testid being the preferred approach where available. Elements are categorized with auth_priority and form_priority flags. The cancellation flow elements are particularly important for subscription management testing. The file includes extraction_stats showing 30 total elements with 1 auth element. Many elements use text-based XPath selectors as fallbacks when data-testid is not available. The page_info section indicates this is from the stage1 environment of HP Instant Ink.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Central configuration file for the AURA test automation framework defining all environment settings, test data, credentials, printer configurations, LLM integration settings, and output paths. Provides locale settings, login URLs, printer inventory with serial numbers and states, Azure OpenAI configuration for AI-driven testing, and directory paths for screenshots, DOM captures, and metadata storage.

locale.description: Locale settings for test execution region and language

login.description: Login credentials and URLs for HP Instant Ink stage environment and HP Smart backup URL

log_file.description: Path to log file for test execution logs

tmp_folder.description: Temporary folder path for intermediate test artifacts

common_test_components.description: Path to shared test components and reusable test modules

large_wait.description: Large wait timeout in minutes, expected to be greater than 15 minutes for long-running operations

metadata.description: Paths to UI control metadata and reference images for visual validation

printers.description: Printer inventory for both PIE and Stage environments with various subscription states for testing different scenarios

llm.description: Azure OpenAI LLM configuration for AI-driven test generation and execution

output.description: Output directory paths for test artifacts including screenshots, DOM snapshots, and metadata files

hints.description: Optional hints to help LLM identify UI elements during AI-driven testing

schema.description: Path to JSON schema file for validation

ai_enabled.description: Flag to enable or disable AI-driven test features

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This YAML configuration file is the central configuration hub for the AURA framework. It defines two test environments (PIE and Stage) with different printer inventories. The printer configurations include various subscription states (subscribed, unsubscribed, out_of_trial_period, initiate_unsubscribe, subscribed_no_pens) enabling comprehensive testing of different user scenarios. The LLM section configures Azure OpenAI GPT-4o integration for AI-driven testing capabilities. Credentials are placeholder values (USERNAME TO BE ADDED, PASSWORD TO BE ADDED, PROVIDE ZURE END POINT, etc.) indicating they should be populated from environment variables or secure storage. The hints section provides guidance to the LLM for element identification. The large_wait value of 16 minutes suggests the framework handles long-running operations like printer enrollment or subscription processing. The selected_printer flag on one printer indicates it's the default printer for test execution.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Configuration loader utility module providing functions to load YAML configuration files and filter printer configurations by description. Serves as the interface between the config.yaml file and test scripts, enabling dynamic printer selection based on subscription state for data-driven testing scenarios.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path to the YAML configuration file to load. Defaults to config.yaml in the current directory.

functions.what_it_does: Loads and parses a YAML configuration file from the specified path. Opens the file in read mode with UTF-8 encoding, uses yaml.safe_load() to parse the YAML content into a Python dictionary, and returns the parsed configuration dictionary. This function provides the primary interface for loading the framework's configuration settings from config.yaml. If the file does not exist or contains invalid YAML syntax, it will raise an exception.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description: Configuration dictionary loaded from config.yaml containing the printers section

functions.what_it_does: Filters and returns printers from the configuration that match the specified description/state. First retrieves the 'printers' list from the config dictionary using config.get('printers', []). Then searches for the 'pie' key in the printers list using next() with a generator expression. Handles two cases: Case 1 - if pie_printers is a single dictionary, checks if its description matches and returns a list containing that printer's model and serial_number. Case 2 - if pie_printers is a list of dictionaries, iterates through all printers and returns a list of dictionaries containing model and serial_number for all printers where the description matches the specified value. Returns an empty list if no printers match or if the pie key is not found. This function enables data-driven testing by allowing test scripts to dynamically select printers based on their subscription state.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This utility module provides two key functions for configuration management. The load_config() function is straightforward and loads YAML files using the safe_load method which prevents arbitrary code execution. The get_printers_by_description() function is more complex and specifically designed to work with the printer configuration structure in config.yaml. It searches for printers in the 'pie' environment section and filters them by description field. The function handles both single printer (dict) and multiple printers (list) scenarios. The description field values correspond to different subscription states used in testing: 'subscribed' for active subscriptions, 'unsubscribed' for cancelled subscriptions, 'out_of_trial_period' for expired trials, 'initiate_unsubscribe' for testing cancellation flow, and 'subscribed_no_pens' for subscriptions without cartridges. This enables parameterized testing where the same test can run against different printer states. The function only searches the 'pie' environment, not 'stage', which suggests PIE is the primary test environment.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Pytest configuration file providing test fixtures, command-line options, and YAML test data loading functionality. Configures logging for test execution, defines custom pytest options for YAML-driven testing, implements YAML file parsing with import expansion for modular test definitions, and provides fixtures for accessing YAML test data and file paths in test functions.

functions.what_it_does: Session-scoped pytest fixture that configures logging for the entire test session. Automatically runs before any tests execute due to autouse=True. First removes any existing log handlers that pytest has already attached by iterating through logging.root.handlers and calling removeHandler() for each. Then configures logging using basicConfig() with INFO level, a timestamp format string showing asctime, levelname, and message. Sets up two handlers: FileHandler writing to the log file path specified in config['log_file'], and StreamHandler writing to sys.stdout for console output. This ensures all test logs are captured both in a file and displayed in the console during test execution.
functions.usage_example: # This fixture runs automatically, no explicit call needed

parameters.name: parser
parameters.description: Pytest command-line parser object for adding custom options

functions.docstring: This hook adds a new command-line option for specifying the YAML file.
functions.what_it_does: Pytest hook function that adds a custom command-line option --yaml-file to the pytest command. Calls parser.addoption() with action='store' to store the provided value, default=None indicating the option is optional, and a help string explaining the option's purpose. This allows test execution to be driven by external YAML files containing test definitions and data. Tests can be run with 'pytest --yaml-file=path/to/test.yaml' to specify which YAML test definition to execute.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Path to the YAML file to load and parse

functions.what_it_does: Loads and parses a YAML file from the specified file path. Opens the file in read mode with UTF-8 encoding using a context manager. Uses yaml.safe_load() to parse the YAML content into a Python dictionary. Returns the parsed dictionary, or an empty dictionary if the file is empty or contains only null. Includes error handling: raises FileNotFoundError with a descriptive message if the file does not exist, and raises ValueError with the YAML parsing error details if the file contains invalid YAML syntax. This function is used internally by other functions to load both main test YAML files and imported component YAML files.

functions.docstring: If the YAML contains flow/steps, return that value. Otherwise return the whole document.
functions.what_it_does: Extracts the flow steps from a YAML data structure. Attempts to access data['flow']['steps'] using nested dictionary access. If successful, returns the steps list. If a KeyError or TypeError occurs (meaning the flow or steps keys do not exist, or data is not a dictionary), catches the exception and returns the entire data object unchanged. This allows the function to handle both structured YAML files with a flow/steps hierarchy and simpler YAML files that contain steps directly at the root level. Used during YAML import expansion to extract the relevant test steps from imported component files.

parameters.name: base_path
parameters.description: Base directory path for resolving relative import file paths

parameters.name: node
parameters.description: YAML node to process - can be a list, dict, or scalar value

functions.docstring: Recursively expand any list items containing a 'file' key by loading the referenced YAML file.
functions.what_it_does: Recursively processes a YAML data structure to expand file imports into inline content. Handles three cases: Case 1 - if node is a list, iterates through each item. If an item is a dictionary containing a 'file' key, constructs the full file path by joining config['common_test_components'] with the file value, prints the file path for debugging, loads the referenced YAML file using load_yaml_file(), extracts its flow steps using extract_flow_steps(), and extends the expanded list with those steps. If an item does not have a 'file' key, recursively processes it and appends the result. Case 2 - if node is a dictionary, recursively processes each value and returns a new dictionary with the same keys and expanded values. Case 3 - if node is a scalar value (string, number, boolean, null), returns it unchanged. This enables modular test definitions where common test components can be defined in separate YAML files and imported into main test files using {file: 'component.yaml'} syntax.

parameters.name: config_path
parameters.description: Path to the main YAML test configuration file

functions.what_it_does: Parses a YAML test flow file with support for multi-phase test definitions and file imports. First determines the base directory of the config file using os.path.dirname() and os.path.abspath(). Loads the root YAML file using load_yaml_file(). Extracts the phases dictionary from root['flow']['phases']. Initializes an empty all_steps list. Iterates through all phase values in the phases dictionary. For each phase's steps list, calls expand_imports() to recursively expand any file imports, then extends all_steps with the expanded steps. Returns a dictionary with structure {'flow': {'steps': all_steps}} containing all test steps from all phases flattened into a single list with all imports expanded. This function is the main entry point for loading YAML-driven test definitions and is called by the yaml_data fixture.

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: Pytest request object providing access to test context and command-line options

functions.docstring: This fixture reads the YAML file specified by the --yaml-file option and returns its content as a Python dictionary.
functions.what_it_does: Pytest fixture that provides parsed YAML test data to test functions. Retrieves the YAML file path from the command-line option using request.config.getoption('--yaml-file'). If no YAML file was provided, skips the test with pytest.skip() and an explanatory message. If a file path was provided, calls parse_flow() to load and parse the YAML file with import expansion. Includes error handling: if the file is not found, fails the test with pytest.fail() and a descriptive message. If YAML parsing fails, fails the test with the parsing error details. Returns the parsed flow dictionary containing all test steps. Test functions can access this data by declaring yaml_data as a parameter.

parameters.name: pytestconfig
parameters.description: Pytest configuration object providing access to command-line options

functions.docstring: Session-scoped fixture that returns the file path passed with --yaml-file.
functions.what_it_does: Session-scoped pytest fixture that provides the YAML file path to test functions. Retrieves the yaml_file option value from pytestconfig using getoption(). If no path was provided (value is None or empty), skips the test with pytest.skip() and a message indicating the option was not provided. If a path was provided, returns it as a string. Being session-scoped means this fixture is evaluated once per test session and the same value is reused across all tests, improving performance. Test functions can access the file path by declaring yaml_file_path as a parameter.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This conftest.py file is a sophisticated pytest configuration module that enables YAML-driven testing in the AURA framework. It provides three key capabilities: 1) Logging configuration that writes to both file and console, 2) Custom --yaml-file command-line option for specifying test definitions, 3) YAML parsing with import expansion allowing modular test composition. The expand_imports() function is particularly powerful - it allows test authors to define reusable test components in separate YAML files and import them using {file: 'component.yaml'} syntax. The parse_flow() function supports multi-phase test definitions where a test can be organized into phases (setup, execution, teardown, etc.) and all phases are flattened into a single steps list. The yaml_data fixture is the main interface for tests to access YAML test definitions. The module loads config.yaml at import time and stores it in a module-level variable, making configuration available to all functions. The framework appears designed for AI-driven testing where test definitions are written in YAML and executed by an AI agent that interprets the steps.

key_reusable_methods: load_config — Loads YAML configuration file and returns parsed dictionary
get_printers_by_description — Filters printers by subscription state for data-driven testing
load_yaml_file — Loads and parses YAML files with error handling
expand_imports — Recursively expands file imports in YAML test definitions
parse_flow — Parses multi-phase YAML test flows with import expansion
configure_logging — Session fixture configuring file and console logging
yaml_data — Fixture providing parsed YAML test data to test functions
pytest_addoption — Adds --yaml-file command-line option to pytest
yaml_file_path — Session fixture providing YAML file path to tests
key_reusable_locators: [data-testid='plan-card'] — Plan selection card on enrollment page
